gpu-performance-portability-requires-kok.title: GPU performance portability requires KOKKOS abstraction layer
gpu-performance-portability-requires-kok.description: Running LAMMPS across heterogeneous GPU/CPU architectures at exascale demands the KOKKOS abstraction. Direct CUDA or HIP code locks simulations to a single vendor. KOKKOS provides portable performance without rewriting kernels.

mlip-spatial-mixing-recovers-accuracy-at.title: MLIP spatial mixing recovers accuracy at reduced computational cost
mlip-spatial-mixing-recovers-accuracy-at.description: High-fidelity MLIPs are too expensive to run everywhere in a simulation box. ML-MIX enables spatial mixing of cheap and expensive potentials, applying the expensive model only where needed (e.g., near defects) for up to 11× speedup.

reactive-md-requires-sub-femtosecond-tim.title: Reactive MD requires sub-femtosecond timesteps for numerical stability
reactive-md-requires-sub-femtosecond-tim.description: ReaxFF and reactive force fields involve bond formation/breaking with stiff potentials. Timesteps of 0.1–0.25 fs are typically required to maintain energy conservation, making reactive simulations 10-40× more expensive per unit time.

mlip-deployment-bottleneck-has-shifted-f.title: MLIP deployment bottleneck has shifted from training to production validation
mlip-deployment-bottleneck-has-shifted-f.description: Training ML interatomic potentials is increasingly automated (DP-GEN, active learning). The real bottleneck is now deploying, validating, benchmarking, and trusting models in production MD runs — especially across different hardware and LAMMPS versions.

model-agnostic-mlip-deployment-enables-c.title: Model-agnostic MLIP deployment enables cross-architecture portability
model-agnostic-mlip-deployment-enables-c.description: Tools like chemtrain-deploy and metatensor provide model-agnostic deployment of MLIPs into standard MD engines. Without this, each potential architecture (MACE, Allegro, DeePMD) requires bespoke integration and GPU dispatch code.

multi-tool-workflow-handoffs-are-the-dom.title: Multi-tool workflow handoffs are the dominant friction in applied MD
multi-tool-workflow-handoffs-are-the-dom.description: Real-world MD workflows chain together Atomsk → LAMMPS → OVITO → Python scripts. Each handoff is manual, brittle, and unreproducible. Even 2025-2026 applied papers show researchers stitching 3-5 tools together without standardized data flow.

lammps-input-script-validation-prevents-.title: LAMMPS input script validation prevents silent simulation errors
lammps-input-script-validation-prevents-.description: Complex LAMMPS input scripts can contain unit mismatches, incompatible fix/pair_style combinations, and missing package dependencies that produce silently wrong results. Static validation (linting) of input scripts is an unmet need across the community.

provenance-capture-is-essential-for-repr.title: Provenance capture is essential for reproducible MD research
provenance-capture-is-essential-for-repr.description: Reproducibility requires capturing the exact LAMMPS version, enabled packages, potential files (with DOI/KIM IDs), GPU settings, compiler flags, and random seeds. Most workflows capture none of this automatically.

streaming-trajectory-analysis-avoids-dum.title: Streaming trajectory analysis avoids dump file sprawl at scale
streaming-trajectory-analysis-avoids-dum.description: Large-scale simulations produce terabytes of dump data. Post-hoc analysis of giant text dumps is I/O-bound and wasteful. Streaming/incremental feature extraction (RDF, MSD, defect counts) during or immediately after simulation is more scalable.

integrator-choice-materially-affects-nem.title: Integrator choice materially affects NEMD simulation outcomes
integrator-choice-materially-affects-nem.description: Seemingly minor details in time integration (SLLOD, Nosé-Hoover chains, damping constants) can materially change viscosity, thermal conductivity, and transport properties in non-equilibrium MD. Documenting integrator choice is critical.

dft-vs-md-cross-validation-establishes-m.title: DFT-vs-MD cross-validation establishes multiscale simulation credibility
dft-vs-md-cross-validation-establishes-m.description: Comparing MD results (energy, forces, structure) against DFT reference calculations is the standard validation procedure for both classical and ML potentials. Without systematic cross-validation, MD predictions lack quantitative credibility.

active-learning-loops-automate-training-.title: Active learning loops automate training data generation for MLIPs
active-learning-loops-automate-training-.description: DP-GEN and similar active learning frameworks identify configurations where the MLIP is uncertain, run DFT on those configurations, and retrain. This avoids redundant training data and systematically fills gaps in chemical/configurational space.

plugin-architecture-resolves-lammps-lice.title: Plugin architecture resolves LAMMPS licensing and distribution constraints
plugin-architecture-resolves-lammps-lice.description: LAMMPS's GPLv2 license creates friction when distributing binaries that include incompatibly-licensed MLIP libraries. The runtime plugin system (dynamic shared objects) allows loading extensions without recompiling, sidestepping license conflicts.

charge-equilibration-adds-significant-co.title: Charge equilibration adds significant complexity to MLIP deployment
charge-equilibration-adds-significant-co.description: Fourth-generation high-dimensional neural network potentials (4G-HDNNPs) require iterative charge equilibration at every MD step. This adds both computational cost and implementation complexity compared to charge-neutral MLIPs.

heat-current-formulations-require-carefu.title: Heat current formulations require careful many-body treatment in MLIPs
heat-current-formulations-require-carefu.description: Computing thermal conductivity via Green-Kubo or NEMD requires correct heat current definitions. Many-body potentials (including MLIPs) introduce non-trivial terms in the heat current that standard two-body formulations miss, leading to systematic errors.

path-integral-md-with-mlips-captures-nuc.title: Path-integral MD with MLIPs captures nuclear quantum effects efficiently
path-integral-md-with-mlips-captures-nuc.description: PIMD combined with ML potentials (e.g., DeePMD) enables quantum nuclear dynamics at near-classical cost. Efficient ring-polymer implementations (fix pimd/langevin) reduce the overhead of running 32+ beads per atom.
